Bio:
Daniel Rodriguez is the Attorney and Owner of Legal Norcal, a law firm based in Chico, California, specializing in estate planning, probate, trust administration, and business law. Admitted to the California Bar in 2017, Rodriguez brings extensive experience in crafting tailored estate plans designed to protect assets, provide for beneficiaries, and minimize tax liabilities. His practice also encompasses business law services, including entity formation, contracts, and business transactions, serving clients throughout Northern California.

Born and raised in Hamilton City, California, Rodriguez's commitment to estate planning stems from personal experience helping his own family navigate the legal complexities following his grandparents' passing. As a first-generation attorney and fluent Spanish speaker, he is dedicated to making legal services accessible to diverse communities. Rodriguez maintains active memberships in WealthCounsel, the National Academy of Elder Law Attorneys (NAELA), and California Advocates for Nursing Home Reform (CANHR), ensuring he stays current with developments in estate planning and elder law to best serve his clients.

Bio:
Sabrina Darwish is the Attorney & Founder of Darwish Law, a criminal defense firm located in Santa Ana, California. Born and raised in Orange County, she earned her undergraduate degree from UCLA and her law degree from Loyola Law School in Los Angeles. Her extensive background has resulted in countless dismissals, acquittals, and successful expungements on behalf of her clients. Known for her compassionate approach and tenacious advocacy, she is committed to fighting for justice and providing experienced criminal defense representation to those in need.

Bio:
Dustin MacFarlane is the Attorney and Owner of California Probate and Trust, PC, based in Fair Oaks, California. A Certified Specialist in Estate Planning, Trust & Probate Law by the State Bar of California Board of Legal Specialization, he focuses his practice on estate planning, probate, and elder law. Before becoming an attorney, MacFarlane worked in the long-term care industry with skilled nursing and assisted living facilities throughout Central Valley and Northern California, giving him unique insight into the needs of families and seniors.

MacFarlane earned his J.D. from the University of Northern California in 2008 and his B.S. in Business Administration in 2004. He has been recognized as a Super Lawyers selection from 2023-2026 and was named a Rising Star from 2014-2019. An active member of the Sacramento County Bar Association's Trusts and Estates Section, the Sacramento Estate Planning Council, and the South Placer Estate Planning Council, he also hosts a weekly show on Talk 650 KSTE and volunteers with the Alzheimer's Association providing legal and financial planning workshops. His firm's mission is to protect the legacies clients have built and ensure their final wishes are carried out without confusion or burden to their families and loved ones.

Bio:
Doug Luther is the Chief Executive Officer and Managing Attorney of Luther Lanard, PC, a franchise law firm based in Plymouth Meeting, Pennsylvania. With approximately 15 years of legal experience, Doug began his career at top-tier law firms including Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP, Glaser Weil Fink Jacobs Howard Avchen & Shapiro LLP, and Mulcahy LLP, where he represented clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies to high-profile individuals. He later served as General Counsel and Chief Compliance Officer at Amada Senior Care from 2018 to 2019, where he built the legal department and managed nationwide franchise expansion, before founding Luther Lanard to focus exclusively on representing franchisees. He earned his J.D. from UCLA School of Law in 2011 with a Business Law and Policy Specialization and was honored as a Dean's Scholar.

Doug specializes in all aspects of franchise law for franchisees, including reviewing franchise disclosure documents, negotiating agreements, resolving disputes involving breach of contract, termination, fraud, and encroachment, and providing general counsel services for franchise operations. He has secured verdicts, judgments, and settlements exceeding $20 million for his clients and has been recognized as a Super Lawyer in Franchise and Dealership Law and a Legal Eagle by Franchise Times. Doug previously chaired the California Lawyers Association's Franchise Law Committee and frequently speaks on franchise law topics, bringing a unique perspective informed by his experience representing both franchisors and franchisees.

Bio:
Peter Detre is an attorney at Munger, Tolles & Olson LLP, a prestigious 200-lawyer firm based in Los Angeles that has been consistently ranked on The American Lawyer's A-List since 2004. With a unique multidisciplinary background, Peter brings exceptional technical expertise to his litigation practice, particularly in complex civil matters and patent litigation representing both patentees and accused infringers across a wide range of technologies.

Peter received his B.S. from McGill University with great distinction in 1980 and earned a Ph.D. in Mathematics from UC Berkeley in 1988. After working as an assistant professor of mathematics for three years, he pursued his legal education at Yale Law School, where he graduated in 1994 and served as senior editor of the Yale Law Journal. Following law school, he clerked for the Honorable Kimba M. Wood in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York before joining Munger, Tolles & Olson LLP.

Bio:
Malcolm Heinicke is a partner at Munger, Tolles & Olson LLP and serves as Co-Managing Partner of the firm. Based in the firm's San Francisco office, he joined Munger Tolles as a lawyer in November 1998 following his clerkship, having previously worked as a summer associate at the firm. His practice focuses on employment and complex commercial litigation, including class actions, employee mobility disputes, wage and hour violations, discrimination, wrongful termination, and trade secrets matters. He represents a diverse roster of clients across financial services, entertainment, utilities, telecommunications, and retail sectors.

Malcolm earned his J.D. from Stanford Law School and an A.B. with honors from Harvard University. He has been recognized as one of the Top Labor and Employment Lawyers in California by the Daily Journal for 13 consecutive years and has been ranked in Band 1 by Chambers USA in Labor & Employment for 14 years. Beyond his legal practice, Malcolm has demonstrated a strong commitment to public service, having served as Chair of the San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency Board of Directors for over 14 years—one of the longest-serving transportation commissioners in the city's history—and previously served as Chair of the San Francisco Human Rights Commission.

Bio:
Amir Freund is a counsel in the IP Litigation practice at Sidley Austin LLP, based in the firm's Palo Alto office. His practice focuses on patent infringement and trade secret litigation involving high-tech hardware and software technologies, including computer networking, telecommunications devices, semiconductors, integrated circuit design, memory circuits, fabrication processes, e-commerce, and digital radio devices. He has extensive experience litigating cases in district courts, the International Trade Commission (ITC), and Inter Partes Review (IPR) proceedings before the USPTO Patent Trial and Appeal Board.

In addition to his litigation work, Amir advises clients on a range of IP-related matters, including patent portfolio management, portfolio analysis for licensing and litigation purposes, and litigation counseling in connection with corporate transactions. He earned his LL.M. in Law, Science, and Technology from Stanford Law School in 2012 and his LL.B from Haifa University in Israel in 2007. Amir is also actively involved in pro bono work related to immigration, foster youth, and identity theft matters.

Bio:
Karen Hawkes is the Principal Attorney and founder of Hawkes Law, APLC (Hawkestrademarklaw), a San Diego-based intellectual property and business law firm. With over 20 years of legal experience, she earned her J.D. from California Western School of Law in 2003 and her B.A. in Political Science from the University of Nevada Las Vegas in 1996. Admitted to the California State Bar in 2005, she is also admitted to practice before the Central District of California and the Southern District of California. Ms. Hawkes began her career in trademark and copyright law at a small intellectual property firm, where she gained extensive hands-on litigation experience from the outset.

As a trademark, copyright, and business attorney, Ms. Hawkes provides both transactional and litigation services to clients ranging from start-ups to well-established corporations. Her practice focuses on full-service trademark representation, including selection, clearance, registration, licensing, and enforcement; copyright registration and infringement litigation; and business law matters such as entity formation, contract drafting, and corporate compliance. She has successfully registered hundreds of trademarks and litigated cases involving federal trademark and copyright infringement, unfair competition, and Lanham Act violations before the Trademark Trial and Appeal Board and in state and federal courts. Ms. Hawkes serves diverse industries including e-commerce, retail, automotive, authors and musicians, software developers, apparel, and craft breweries.

Bio:

Bio:
Marshall Waller is the Managing Shareholder and Senior Attorney at Feinberg & Waller, APC, a distinguished family law firm serving Southern California with offices in Calabasas and Beverly Hills. A California Certified Family Law Specialist (CFLS), Mr. Waller has been admitted to the California State Bar since 1981 and leads a skilled team of attorneys who specialize exclusively in family law matters, including divorce, child custody, support, property division, prenuptial agreements, and international child abduction cases. His extensive expertise encompasses high-asset property division, complex jurisdictional conflicts, and interstate and international family law matters.

Mr. Waller is a Fellow of both the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and the International Academy of Family Lawyers, reflecting his standing in the legal community. He is the co-author of the widely respected treatise *California Family Law*, now in its ninth edition and published by Wolters Kluwer, which is used by law schools, legal professionals, and courts throughout the state. An active legal educator, Mr. Waller served as an Adjunct Law Professor at Pepperdine University School of Law and frequently lectures on family law for continuing legal education programs. He holds a J.D. from Loyola Law School, completed postgraduate tax studies at USC and USD, and earned his B.A. with Highest Honors from California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo.

Bio:
Gary D. Sparks is the President and Attorney at Sparks Family Law, Inc., a full-service family law, divorce, and estate planning firm based in Walnut Creek, California. With over a decade of experience, Gary founded the firm in 2009 after dedicating himself to family law following his own highly contested divorce. His personal experience drives his passion for helping clients navigate complex family-centered legal issues with compassion and aggressive advocacy. Prior to establishing his own practice, Gary was a partner at Holmes, Moore & Sparks, a well-known family law firm with over 25 years of East Bay advocacy experience.

Gary has successfully represented hundreds of clients, including husbands, wives, parents, military service members, and same-sex couples, in matters ranging from divorce and child custody to spousal support, property division, and domestic violence cases. The firm also provides estate planning services, including wills, trusts, powers of attorney, and advance health care directives, as well as family law mediation services. A graduate of California State University with a B.S. degree, Gary is an active member of the State Bar of California, American Bar Association, and multiple county bar associations. He regularly shares his expertise through appearances on KGO-AM 810 in San Francisco, conducts pro-per divorce clinics, and volunteers as a "Lawyer in the Library" to assist self-represented parties, demonstrating his commitment to making family law accessible to all Californians.
Title: President / Attorney
Company Location: 2890 North Main Street, Walnut Creek, CA 94597, United States
Company Description:
Sparks Family Law, Inc. is a full-service family law, divorce, and estate planning firm dedicated to helping you navigate and resolve family-centered legal issues. Our goal is to empower you with knowledge and provide exceptional legal services to help you understand and assert your rights under California law. The firm was founded by attorney Gary D. Sparks, who dedicated himself many years ago to becoming a family lawyer after undergoing his own highly contested divorce case.

We treat all of our clients with care and compassion, and advocate aggressively for their rights. Whether it be the financial issues of child and spousal support, retirement and property division or the parental custody issues of creating a safe and sane environment in which you and your children can thrive, we will guide you through this unsettling time and work towards achieving the solutions that you need and deserve.

We focus our practice on family-centered law, including divorce, legal separation, annulment, paternity, termination of domestic partnerships and same sex marriages, military divorce, child custody and visitation, guardianships, child support, spousal support (aka alimony), community property and debt division, retirement plan division, domestic violence, and other post-Judgment issues. We also offer family estate planning services, such as preparing wills, trusts, powers of attorney and advance health care directives, and in the near future will begin offering probate services to assist the families of those who have passed away. In addition to litigation, we also provide family law mediation services.

We are an experienced, highly competent firm that is supportive of your concerns and will advocate for your rights, all while balancing the rigorous demands of the California legal system. We will advise you of your various options, discuss the pros and cons of each option, and walk you through the range of potential outcomes in your case.

Bio:
Peter Haven is the Attorney and Owner of Haven Law, a solo practice located in Manhattan Beach, California. As a trial attorney and litigator, he specializes in a diverse range of legal matters including real estate, business litigation, personal injury, criminal defense, and family law. Licensed to practice in California since 1994 (Bar #175048), Peter has built over two decades of experience handling cases across both civil and criminal proceedings.

Peter earned his J.D. from UCLA School of Law in 1994 and his A.B. from Stanford University in 1987. Prior to establishing Haven Law, he worked as an Associate at Musick Peeler & Garrett from 1994 to 2000. Recognized as a Rising Stars selection by Super Lawyers in 2004 and noted as a top-rated Business Litigation attorney in Manhattan Beach, Peter is known for his aggressive litigation approach and expert knowledge across multiple practice areas. He provides trusted legal representation at reasonable rates, drawing on his extensive courtroom experience to deliver effective solutions for his clients.

Title: C.E.O.
Company Location: 4141 Hacienda Drive, Pleasanton, CA 94588, United States
Company Description:
Perfect for small to medium-sized practices, ChARM EHR is an affordable web-based EHR, Practice Management and Medical Billing solution that can be accessed from anywhere at anytime using an internet ready computer, iPad, or tablet. ChARM EHR is highly customizable and intuitive, improving practice workflow and patient interactions. 

The ChARM patient portal, an integral part of the system, is accessible as soon as the patient is registered. Patient waiting time is reduced when they can use it to request future appointments, share medical history documents, and complete pre-appointment paperwork. ChARM also offers a convenient patient check-in kiosk application in which information is instantly transferred to the patient chart. 

Other features available in ChARM EHR include an advanced appointment scheduler, SOAP notes and templates, a comprehensive medical billing application, e-prescribing module, secure physician messaging, and even an inventory management module. It is HIPPA and HITECH Act compliant, as well as ONC-ATCB certified. 

Unlike most EHR and PM systems, ChARM EHR is priced per patient encounter. It’s flexible and can support unlimited providers. Because it is a SaaS solution, the need to purchase and maintain hardware is eliminated and there are no upfront costs. ChARM EHR is hosted on the popular Zoho cloud which supports more than 7 million users and adheres to industry standards for security and privacy. 

ChARM EHR is great to consider if you’re looking for a user-friendly EHR and PM solution that will improve efficiencies and the workflow of your office.

Title: Dental Office Manager
Company Location: 530 South Main Street, Orange, CA 92868, United States
Company Description:
Western Dental & Orthodontics (including affiliates such as Brident & Vital Smiles) is one of the nation’s largest dental providers and the leader in accessible, affordable oral healthcare.  With 350+ affiliated offices throughout CA, AL, TX, AZ, and NV, our network includes over 1200 dentists in which over 300 of them are Board Specialists.  In California, we are the leading provider of services to both adults and children in the Medi-Cal Dental public insurance program that provides health care benefits for more than 13 million low-income individuals and families.
In addition to general dentistry, Western Dental offers orthodontics, oral surgery, pediatric dentistry, periodontics and endodontics. All services are backed by a unique quality assurance system that electronically monitors all patient visits, treatments and clinical performance to enable high-quality care.
Our Corporate Offices based in Orange, CA provide support to affiliated offices throughout various departments including: Operations, Human Resources, Marketing, Procurement, IT, Facilities, Revenue Cycle Management, Accounting, Quality Management, and more.
We’ve been making quality care more affordable since our founder, Dr. Curtis Beauchamp, opened his first office in 1903.  A true visionary, he opened up dentistry to the people and championed the idea that everyone should have good oral health.  He did it by delivering excellent care at affordable prices and letting his patients pay over time. Back then, they called it "Credit Dentistry.”  These days, it's how almost all of us afford the braces, root canals and implants we need.  By the 1930’s Dr. Beauchamp and his son, Dr. Robert F. Beauchamp, were treating thousands of patients at multiple locations.  Fast forward to today, and we serve over 1 million people a year in five states.  As a true Dental Home, we treat patients of all ages through our general and multi-specialty platform.

Title: Senior Collections Manager

Company Description:
Western Dental & Orthodontics (including affiliates such as Brident & Vital Smiles) is one of the nation’s largest dental providers and the leader in accessible, affordable oral healthcare.  With 350+ affiliated offices throughout CA, AL, TX, AZ, and NV, our network includes over 1200 dentists in which over 300 of them are Board Specialists.  In California, we are the leading provider of services to both adults and children in the Medi-Cal Dental public insurance program that provides health care benefits for more than 13 million low-income individuals and families.
In addition to general dentistry, Western Dental offers orthodontics, oral surgery, pediatric dentistry, periodontics and endodontics. All services are backed by a unique quality assurance system that electronically monitors all patient visits, treatments and clinical performance to enable high-quality care.
Our Corporate Offices based in Orange, CA provide support to affiliated offices throughout various departments including: Operations, Human Resources, Marketing, Procurement, IT, Facilities, Revenue Cycle Management, Accounting, Quality Management, and more.
We’ve been making quality care more affordable since our founder, Dr. Curtis Beauchamp, opened his first office in 1903.  A true visionary, he opened up dentistry to the people and championed the idea that everyone should have good oral health.  He did it by delivering excellent care at affordable prices and letting his patients pay over time. Back then, they called it "Credit Dentistry.”  These days, it's how almost all of us afford the braces, root canals and implants we need.  By the 1930’s Dr. Beauchamp and his son, Dr. Robert F. Beauchamp, were treating thousands of patients at multiple locations.  Fast forward to today, and we serve over 1 million people a year in five states.  As a true Dental Home, we treat patients of all ages through our general and multi-specialty platform.

A true visionary, he opened up dentistry to the people and championed the idea that everyone should have good oral health.  He did it by delivering excellent care at affordable prices and letting his patients pay over time. Back then, they called it "Credit Dentistry.”  These days, it's how almost all of us afford the braces, root canals and implants we need.  By the 1930’s Dr. Beauchamp and his son, Dr. Robert F. Beauchamp, were treating thousands of patients at multiple locations.  Fast forward to today, and we serve over 1 million people a year in five states.  As a true Dental Home, we treat patients of all ages through our general and multi-specialty platform.

Benefit Recovery Partners has specialized in providing addition treatment centers and related healthcare professionals with results driven insurance billing and claims management services since 2008.

Sadly, there continues to be a disconnect between the clinical, treatment, billing and executive teams, especially with outsourced billing services. This results in lost days, denials, overlooked claims and increasing AR, but not with Benefit Recovery Partners.

Benefit Recovery Partners has a team who are highly experienced and extremely effective at what they do and who act more like your in-house department instead of an outsourced and distant service. We understand how important communication is for success.
If you have any concerns or questions with your current revenue cycle management, we would be happy to talk with you and provide a no cost, no pressure evaluation of where and how we can help.

Company Description:
Neuromonitoring Technologies, Inc. (NMT) is the leading provider of intra-operative, on-site, and EMU/ICU neuromonitoring services.  Neuromonitoring Technologies, Inc (NMT) is a privately held company that specializes in on-site EEG services and ICU/EMU neuromonitoring for quick recognition of brain function changes for immediate intervention in the ICU and EMU.  NMT also provides Intra-operative Neurological Monitoring (IONM) during surgical procedures that place the central and peripheral nervous system at risk.  
NMT was founded in 1998 and currently employs 30+ clinical neurophysiologists as well as five board-certified neurologists that, combined, perform over six thousand IONM procedures annually.  In 2012 NMT added staff to offer their expertise in neuromonitoring, specializing in continuous EEG monitoring for neurosurgical, pediatric, neonatal, surgical and general medicine ICUs. In 2017, NMT began providing supplemental and primary on-site EEG services to hospitals and neurology clinics. 
NMT provides IONM services at fifty-four hospitals for over two hundred surgeons throughout the Mid-Atlantic region including Maryland, District of Columbia, Pennsylvania, Virginia, Delaware and New Jersey.  Through telemedicine capabilities, NMT has made available their ICU neuromonitoring expertise to ICUs across the country for rapid assessment of brain function.  NMT is exploring international telemedicine opportunities providing cEEG in the ICU setting.

Title: Neurophysiologist
Company Location: Po Box 320171, Los Gatos, CA 95032, United States
Company Description:
Collectively we share a common belief; the patient deserves the best possible care before, during and after surgery. 
When neuromonitoring is indicated, the assigned neurophysiologist plays an important role in their healthcare during surgery. Intraoperative neuromonitoring brings state-of-the-art technology into the operating room and uses electrophysiological techniques to monitor the patient’s nervous system throughout the surgical procedure.
The role of the neurophysiologist in the operating room is not a passive one. Observing the continuous collection of data requires vigilance in order to rapidly assimilate, interpret and convey to the surgeon in real time a well thought out analysis of the ongoing events.
This is a collaborative effort between the neurophysiologist present in the operating room and the online physician providing continuous remote observation and interpretation of the data. Because the surgeon is receiving real time feedback during surgery, events that previously went undetected until the patient was in post-operative recovery can now be corrected immediately.
It goes without saying, neuromonitoring is effective as a predictor of post operative complications.
Patients exhibiting unresolved changes in the evoked responses recorded during surgery will present with post operative deficits. A more important question is whether neuromonitoring is able to reduce the incidence of injury to the nervous system during surgery.

Invitrx Therapeutics, Inc. is a biotechnology company specializing in the culture and engineering of adult stem cells by which we develop innovative products and therapies that are used in aesthetics, wound closure, and healing as well as plastic and reconstructive surgery. 

Invitrx was founded in July 2003 by Habib Torfi following many years of research and clinical trials involving adult stem cell technology. The research was originally developed to treat patients suffering from burns, diabetic ulcers, ocular surface disorders, and other ophthalmic conditions.

Methods were developed to harvest and concentrate stem cells, providing the bases for Invitrx's cytokine and growth factor technology that demonstrated remarkable results in anti-aging by reducing the appearance of blemishes, fine lines, and wrinkles. Thus, using Invitrx stem cell core technologies, the ReLuma skin, hair, and lash therapy product line was created.

ReLuma cosmetic products were designed utilizing adult stem cell technology to rejuvenate and reactivate dormant cells. The entire line of ReLuma products, including hair care, are formulated with human adult stem cell conditioned media containing cytokines and growth factors (along with interleukins and matrix proteins) to help reduce signs of aging and promote a youthful, even-toned glow.

Title: Senior Associate General Counsel
Company Location: 1 Healthcare Way, Lakewood, NJ 08701, United States
Company Description:
Knipper Health is a leading force in the healthcare industry, serving the pharmaceutical, biotechnology, & medical device industries for more than 35 years. Our mission is to help our clients manage their brands anywhere in the product life cycle for enhanced patient well-being. We purpose-build answers specifically to address a comprehensive range of client challenges from marketing and sample management (MSM) to third-party logistics (3PL) to custom pharmacy solutions (CPS). 
An acknowledged leader in the sample management channel, Knipper MSM highlights Samplicity—the industry’s only fully integrated sample management platform, which features a one resource, one team, one point-of-contact approach, & a unique suite of operational services & technical advancements. 
KnippeRx, Custom Pharmacy Solutions (CPS) is a highly nimble, independent specialty pharmacy that serves both commercial and non-commercial needs. KnippeRx helps overcome the complex barriers to patient access by providing the shortest path from initiation of a prescription to delivery of essential therapy.
Knipper 3PL provides advanced secure warehousing and distribution in life sciences. Solutions include Knipper SEAL™ (Secure Exchange of Authentic Logistics), an array of overlapping systems and processes to ensure the secure handling of your products from receiving through distribution and after delivery to your buyer. FDA/GMP and validated process controls, NAPD/VAWD and AICPS SOC accredited operations. Full support of DSCSA/DQSA, including offering full serialization system management and child-to-parent aggregation of unaggregated inventory for November 27, 2023 requirement. Cold-chain and tracking, DEA storage, flash-title to facilitate market entry, tamper evident shipment options, highly secure facilities and network, backup systems and redundancies, and more. A one-stop resource to help bring out a brand’s full potential, while elevating the patient experience to new levels of excellence.

Marts&Lundy serves as a trusted partner to mission-driven organizations whose bold aspirations are realized through the power of philanthropy. Our fundraising consultants bring an unmatched depth and breadth of expertise and services to client engagements. Title: Senior Intellectual Property Counsel
Contact Location: San Juan Capistrano, CA, United States
Company Location: 500 Plaza Drive, Secaucus, NJ 07094, United States
Company Description:
Quest Diagnostics (NYSE: DGX) empowers people to take action to improve health outcomes.  Derived from the world's largest database of clinical lab results, our diagnostic insights reveal new avenues to identify and treat disease, inspire healthy behaviors and improve health care management.  Quest annually serves one in three adult Americans and half the physicians and hospitals in the United States, and our 47,000 employees understand that, in the right hands and with the right context, our diagnostic insights can inspire actions that transform lives.
The company offers physicians the broadest test menu (3,000+ tests), is a pioneer in developing innovative new tests, is the leader in cancer diagnostics, provides anatomic pathology (AP) services, & interpretive consultation through its medical & scientific staff of about 900 M.D.s & Ph.D.s. The company reported 2020 revenues of $9.44 billion. 
Quest Diagnostics offers the most extensive clinical testing network in the U.S., with laboratories in most major metropolitan areas, & in Mexico, the UK & India. The company also operates four esoteric laboratories, 40 outpatient AP laboratories, & 160 smaller, rapid-response laboratories. 
Patients may have specimens collected in any of the company’s approximately 2,250 patient service centers. On a typical workday, testing is performed for about 550,000 patients. 
Quest Diagnostics empowers healthcare organizations & clinicians with state-of-the-art connectivity solutions. 
The company is the leading provider of pre-employment drugs-of-abuse screening for employers & risk assessment services for the life insurance industry. It is the world’s 2nd largest provider of clinical trials testing for new pharmaceuticals.
More information is available at www.questdiagnostics.com.

Title: Associate Attorney
Company Location: 55 Challenger Road, Ridgefield Park, NJ 07660, United States
Company Description:
One of the nation’s preeminent plaintiffs’ law firms, Seeger Weiss is best known for multidistrict mass torts and class actions in both state and federal court. From offices in New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and Massachusetts, the firm has represented over 10,000 individuals, companies, and governments across the U.S. who have been injured or defrauded on a massive scale.
Since its founding in 1999, it has led many of the most complex and high-profile cases in the country: the National Prescription Opiate Litigation, which the Washington Post called “the largest federal court case in U.S. history”; 3M Combat Arms Earplug Products Liability Litigation, which the Minneapolis Star Tribune called “one of the largest mass torts ever”; the ongoing “Dieselgate” scandal; the sprawling multistate litigation on behalf of survivors of child sexual abuse; and the history-making Football League Players’ Concussion Injury Litigation.
Unlike many plaintiffs’ firms, Seeger Weiss doesn’t simply “collect” clients for other lawyers to serve. We go to court and get results, regularly winning amounts in hundreds of millions and billions of dollars on behalf of large classes of injured clients. Whatever the case, we have the experience and resources necessary to go toe-to-toe with the biggest and best-funded corporations in America.
We are proud to share the Seeger Weiss Diversity Fellowship as a part of our ongoing commitment to recruiting, retaining, and promoting attorneys from underrepresented backgrounds. 
https://www.seegerweiss.com/diversity/
Prior results do not guarantee or predict a similar outcome in any future matter.

Title: Lawyer
Company Location: 70 South Orange Avenue, Livingston, NJ 07039, United States
Company Description: After I had been working with my company for several years, I finally got up the nerve to ask my boss for a promotion. He offered me a great position, but the only caveat was that it was at another branch of the company that was practically across the country! He let me think about it for a while, and by the time I told him I would take it, he told me that they were in desperate need of me and I had to move in just a week! I had never made such a quick move before and started panicking. I was very lucky to get settled in my new city with enough time to explore before I began working. I want to help others facing quick moves to advance their careers like I did, so I thought I would share my moving tips on a blog!

Company Description:
Jacobs, Schwalbe & Petruzzelli, P.C., is a well–established, full-service law firm providing experienced counsel in workers' compensation, Social Security disability, personal injury and employment law since 1977. The firm's reputation and law practice are built upon integrity, compassion and unsurpassed attorney expertise.

We've seen the effects of work injuries on decent, hardworking people — physical, psychological and financial. That's why we won't allow anyone to take advantage of them.

Our firm seamlessly integrates and pursues our clients' claims smoothly, from start to settlement. For example, an on-the-job injury can produce separate negligence, workers' compensation and social security disability claims. This might be the case if a construction worker is seriously injured on a job site and the negligence can be traced back to a third party (someone other than the employer or a co-employer). Our experienced attorneys can evaluate your specific circumstances and help with all of your legal options.
